drafts.text:
Title: I keep seeing founders spend a week on domain names. Made a free generator + a 7-min guide on the 4 traps that kill founder-picked names.

Body: Spent the last few days obsessing over our SaaS domain (we shipped "AlphaForge" — terrible, renamed) and realized the actual decision is way simpler than founders make it. The 5 traits: short, pronounceable, brandable, trademark-safe, right TLD. The 4 traps: inside jokes, misspelled real words, scale-proofing too early, hyphens and numbers.

Wrote it up + built a free generator that checks RDAP availability live: tinytools-smoky.vercel.app/domain-generator/

What's the worst startup name you've seen survive anyway?

1/ Founders waste 2 weeks on domain names. The decision is 30 minutes if you follow the framework. Here's what works in 2026:

2/ The 5 traits every great SaaS domain has:
• Short (under 12 chars)
• Pronounceable (say it at a bar test)
• Brandable > descriptive
• Trademark-safe (USPTO TESS, 5 min)
• Right TLD (.com still wins, .ai works for AI products)

3/ The 4 traps I see kill founder-picked names:
• Inside jokes (funny to 3 people, not customers)
• Misspelled real words (Tumblr survived; 50 dead startups didn't)
• Scale-proofing too early ("Platform.com")
• Hyphens and numbers (always reads as cheap knockoff)

4/ The myth that "all good .coms are taken" is 90% false. Pronounceable, brandable .com domains are everywhere — just not as dictionary words. Spotify, Slack, Roblox didn't exist as English words.

5/ Built a free domain generator that checks live RDAP availability — no signup, no email. Brandable, descriptive, modern, compound styles. 60+ candidates per query.

tinytools-smoky.vercel.app/domain-generator/

Founders waste two weeks on domain names. The decision is 30 minutes.

The 5 traits every great SaaS domain has:
• Short (under 12 chars — Linear, Vercel, Cursor are all 6 letters)
• Pronounceable (the "say it at a noisy bar" test)
• Brandable beats descriptive (Notion ate the category, BestProjectManagementApp.com never could)
• Trademark-safe (USPTO TESS, 5 minutes, cheap insurance)
• Right TLD (.com still wins. .ai for AI. Avoid .biz/.info/.xyz.)

The 4 traps that quietly kill founder-picked names:
• Inside jokes that aren't funny to customers
• Misspelled real words (for every Tumblr there are 50 dead startups)
• Scale-proofing too early ("Platform.com")
• Hyphens and numbers (always read as cheap knockoff)

Built a free generator that checks live RDAP availability for 60+ candidates per query — no signup, no email.

Full guide + free tool: tinytools-smoky.vercel.app/domain-generator/

Title: Show HN: Free domain name generator with live RDAP availability checks (no signup)

First comment to post in the discussion: Built this because every existing domain generator I tried (1) gated results behind email signup, (2) showed "available" for domains that were actually parked, or (3) only suggested .com and demanded an upsell for .ai/.io. Mine: pure browser, RDAP-based real-time check (so it's honest), four naming styles (brandable, descriptive, compound, modern), affiliate link to Namecheap on the few that the user clicks (in review, not earning yet). Open to feedback on the algorithms — happy to add more naming styles.
